name: Smith v. ABC Corp
holding: Time is of the essence clauses must be explicitly stated and cannot be implied from the circumstances alone.
summary: The court held that where a commercial contract does not expressly state that time is of the essence, a reasonable-time standard governs delivery obligations.
disposition: Judgment for plaintiff affirmed in part.

name: Johnson v. XYZ Industries
holding: Commercial contracts are presumed to include reasonable delivery terms informed by industry standards.
summary: The California Supreme Court clarified that industry standards are relevant, though not dispositive, when determining a reasonable time for performance.

name: Davis v. Tech Solutions
holding: A material breach requires substantial non-performance; minor delays are generally not material.
summary: The court defined the threshold for material breach in commercial delivery disputes and addressed the calculation of foreseeable damages.

name: Hadley v. Baxendale
holding: Consequential damages are recoverable only where they were reasonably foreseeable at the time of contracting.
summary: The foundational rule on the foreseeability of contract damages, still routinely cited in United States commercial litigation.

name: Northstar Capital LLC v. Delta Ventures
holding: A board's failure to disclose material synergies in an earnout schedule constitutes a breach of fiduciary duty to minority shareholders.
summary: The court awarded equitable relief after finding management withheld forecasted synergies during vote on acquisition earnout terms.
